headline: Washington and USC meet as co-ranked teams seeking redemption
subhead: Both squads stumbled last week and need to prove their ranking credentials in this pivotal West Coast clash.

xFactor.why: Washington's ability to generate pressure without blitzing could disrupt USC's rhythm and force the kind of mistake the Huskies nearly capitalized on against Oregon.

keyMatchup: Washington's secondary coverage versus USC's receiver separation in vertical routes will determine field position and third-down conversions.
body: Both teams arrive wounded. Washington had Oregon's final drive in hand before a late fade allowed the Ducks to escape, while USC couldn't contain Miami's passing attack in the fourth quarter. These aren't flaws that erase what got each program to 2-1, but they expose vulnerabilities that the opponent across the field will probe relentlessly.

The staff that adjusts faster will move to 3-1. Washington's recent loss came down to clock management and one defensive lapse late; USC's came down to inconsistent secondary communication. On neutral ground at the Coliseum, edge possession and field position become currency. Washington's experience closing tight games—even in failure—gives it a slight edge in a matchup where both teams are equally talented and equally desperate to stabilize.
